data.text: I’ll use the `frontend-design` skill because this bead changes Svelte UI and screenshots are part of the acceptance criteria. I’m starting by reading the relevant skill and project files before editing.

The frontend skill is general style guidance; this bead is constrained by existing tests and DDx UI patterns, so I’m keeping the implementation aligned with the repo rather than inventing a new visual system. Next I’m locating the Svelte routes and plugin test expectations.

The GraphQL constants already include plugin list/detail/dispatch and a preview fallback, but there are no plugin routes yet. I’m reading adjacent project pages now so the new routes match the existing load and component conventions.

I have enough context to implement the bead now. I’m adding project-scoped plugin list and detail routes, wiring them to the existing GraphQL constants, adding the sidebar link, and then I’ll update the screenshot coverage for the new pages.

The first implementation pass is in place. I’m adding plugin pages to the visual regression screenshot list and making the preview fallback show the same representative plugin states used by the e2e tests.

The routes and screenshot definitions are written. I’m running the frontend type checker next because Svelte 5 syntax and generated route types tend to catch wiring mistakes before the browser tests do.

`bun run check` failed immediately because frontend dependencies are not installed in this worktree (`svelte-kit` is missing). I’m checking the package state and will install from the lockfile if needed.
The frontend `node_modules` directory is absent, so I’m installing dependencies from `bun.lock` before rerunning checks and e2e.
Type checking is green, with only warnings. I’m cleaning up the new accessibility warnings on the detail regions before running the Playwright plugin tests.
The new Svelte code type-checks now; Prettier found formatting changes in the new route files and screenshot spec, so I’m applying the repo formatter to those files only.
Formatting is applied. I’m running the exact acceptance test file now: `e2e/plugins.spec.ts`.

The plugin spec exposed three concrete issues: duplicate text made strict locators ambiguous, the shared dialog close button name matched confirm locators, and the radio input needs stable dimensions for Playwright’s `check()`. I’m tightening those now.
The ambiguity fixes compile cleanly. I’m rerunning `e2e/plugins.spec.ts` to verify the four acceptance tests.
The previous failed Playwright run left the preview server on port 4173. I’m locating that process, stopping it, then rerunning the plugin spec.

Two issues remain: the visible Update button text collides with the status-badge text locator, and the radio’s actual input geometry is bad even though the dialog looks right in the accessibility snapshot. I’m going to make Update icon-only with an accessible label, then inspect the radio bounding box against a running preview.

The radio failure is from the shared dialog not actually being centered: Tailwind is not emitting the positioning utilities used by `ConfirmDialog`, so the bottom of the modal falls outside the viewport. I’m moving those critical geometry values to explicit inline styles on the dialog content.
The dialog geometry fix compiles. I’m rerunning the four plugin e2e tests now.
The plugin acceptance spec is green: 4/4 passed. I’m generating the committed light and dark Playwright snapshots for the plugin list and detail pages now.

The screenshot update raced a stale preview shutdown: tests started before the new preview was ready, so only one image was written. I verified port 4173 is clear and am rerunning the focused screenshot update.
The focused screenshot update passed and wrote the new plugin list/detail light and dark snapshot files. I’m checking the worktree now so I can verify only intentional files are staged and committed.

The build artifact was generated by verification, not part of the bead, so I restored it. I’m doing one final focused screenshot regression run without `--update-snapshots` to prove the committed images match.
The post-update screenshot check hit the same preview-start race, not a pixel mismatch. I’m clearing the port, pausing briefly, and rerunning once more from a clean server state.

The remaining dirty frontend files are intentional, and the unrelated `.agents` / `.claude` changes are outside this bead. I’m staging only the plugin UI, dialog fix, screenshot spec, and the four new snapshot PNGs.
The staged set is clean and limited to this bead’s frontend work plus screenshots. I’m committing it with the required bead tag now.

Committed `5bd2d60b feat: add plugin registry UI [ddx-4cecb7d5]`.

Implemented plugin list/detail routes, YAML manifest rendering, install/update/uninstall dispatch flows, sidebar navigation, preview fallback data, and committed light/dark plugin screenshots.

Verification:
- `bun run check` passed
- `bunx playwright test e2e/plugins.spec.ts` passed, 4/4
- `bunx playwright test e2e/screenshots.spec.ts -g "plugins|plugin-detail"` passed, 4/4

Only pre-existing unrelated `.agents` / `.claude` worktree changes remain unstaged.
